# Budget Feature Shadow Spec

> Budget Calculation - "Safe to Spend" computation with spending pool, rollover, and daily velocity display

**Status:** Planned
**Generated by:** spec-lead + @pseudo-worker

---

## Problem Statement

Users see their Safe to Spend balance as a lump sum but have no intuitive sense of whether that amount is healthy or tight given where they are in the month. A user with ₱10,000 STS on the 28th of the month is in a very different position from one with ₱10,000 on the 1st — but the current UI shows them the same number. Without a per-day context, users cannot make confident day-to-day spending decisions. If we don't surface daily velocity, users will either over-spend early in the month or under-spend unnecessarily late in the month.

## Target User

A daily active user who checks the home screen before making discretionary purchases. They already understand their STS balance but want a quick "am I on track?" signal without doing mental math. They are not financial experts — the number should be immediately interpretable: "I can spend ₱X today."

## Success Metrics

- Daily velocity value is always dynamically computed from `daysRemainingInMonth` — hardcoded divisor (`/ 30`) is eliminated entirely
- Last-day-of-month warning is shown on exactly the final day of every calendar month, no false positives
- Layout change (daily rate below STS) is confirmed in Pencil before and after implementation
- Zero regressions in `CalculateSafeToSpendUseCase` or `HeroSafeToSpend` existing tests

## Out of Scope

- Changing the STS calculation formula itself (this is display-only)
- Weekly or yearly velocity views
- Historical velocity tracking or trends
- Sandbox mode daily velocity (sandbox does not show this component in its current layout)
- Push notifications based on daily velocity

## Non-Functional Requirements

- **Performance:** `daysRemainingInMonth()` is a pure, local computation — no DB call, no network. Must be synchronous.
- **Reliability:** Must handle month-end boundary correctly on all calendar months (28/29/30/31 days). Must use device local timezone, consistent with how `DateUtils` handles all other date operations.
- **Accessibility:** The (!) warning icon + red text on last day must not rely on color alone — icon is required as a second signal. Tooltip must be dismissible.
- **Correctness:** `daysRemainingInMonth` always includes today as a remaining day. On the last day of the month the divisor is 1 and the daily rate equals the full STS balance.

## Architecture

```sudolang
// Package-by-Feature Structure
// src/commonMain/kotlin/com/budgetcalendar/features/budget/
// ├── domain/
// │   ├── model/           # SpendingPool, BudgetSummary, MonthlyRollover
// │   └── usecase/        # CalculateSpendingPool, ApplyRollover
// └── presentation/
//     └── BudgetViewModel.kt
//
// Utility touched:
// core/utils/DateUtils.kt  — add daysRemainingInMonth()
//
// Component touched:
// core/designsystem/component/HeroSafeToSpend.kt — layout + warning state
```

---

## Data Model

```sudolang
// Domain Models
interface SpendingPool {
  accounts: List<Account>;
  totalBalance: Long;           // Sum of all spending pool accounts (in cents)
  pendingReservations: Long;    // Sum of PENDING transactions (in cents)
  overdueReservations: Long;   // Sum of OVERDUE transactions (in cents)
  confirmedThisMonth: Long;     // Sum of CONFIRMED transactions this month (in cents)
  availableToSpend: Long;      // Real-time "Safe to Spend" (in cents)
}

interface BudgetSummary {
  totalLiquidAssets: Long;      // Sum of all spending pool accounts
  pendingReservations: Long;    // Sum of PENDING transactions
  overdueReservations: Long;    // Sum of OVERDUE transactions
  confirmedSpending: Long;      // Sum of CONFIRMED this month
  availableToSpend: Long;      // Real-time "Safe to Spend"
  rolloverAmount: Long;         // Unspent from previous month
  creditCardReserved: Long;     // Reserved for CC payments
  lastCalculatedAt: Long;
}

interface MonthlyRollover {
  id: Long;
  year: Int;
  month: Int;
  rolloverAmount: Long;
  createdAt: Long;
}

interface CreditCardReservation {
  accountId: Long;
  accountName: String;
  reservedAmount: Long;
  pendingTransactions: List<Transaction>;
}

// UI State Models
interface BudgetUiState {
  budgetSummary: BudgetSummary;
  spendingPoolAccounts: List<Account>;
  creditCardReservations: List<CreditCardReservation>;
  isLoading: Boolean;
  isCalculating: Boolean;
  error: String?;
}
```

---

## Constraints

```sudolang
Constraint: Safe to Spend = sum(balances of accounts where isInSpendingPool == true)
Constraint: Pending transactions are deducted from Safe to Spend
Constraint: Cancelled transactions are NOT deducted
Constraint: Overdue transactions remain deducted until confirmed or cancelled
Constraint: Monthly rollover: unspent Safe to Spend carries to next month
Constraint: Rollover amount = Safe to Spend at end of month - confirmed expenses
Constraint: Sandbox mode Safe to Spend is calculated independently
Constraint: Credit Card expenses are deducted immediately when incurred
Constraint: Calculation updates in real-time when transactions change state
Constraint: Rollover history is preserved for financial reporting
Constraint: All monetary amounts stored in cents (Long)

// Daily Velocity Constraints
Constraint: dailyRate = availableToSpend / daysRemainingInMonth
Constraint: daysRemainingInMonth = (lastDayOfMonth - today.dayOfMonth + 1), using device local timezone
Constraint: daysRemainingInMonth always >= 1 (today is always counted as a remaining day)
Constraint: dailyRate is only shown when availableToSpend > 0
Constraint: On the last day of the month (daysRemainingInMonth == 1), the warning state is active
Constraint: Daily rate divisor must NEVER be hardcoded to 30
```

---

## Data Flow

```sudolang
// Calculate Safe to Spend Flow
Trigger: App start, transaction change, or manual refresh
  |> GetSpendingPoolAccountsUseCase.execute()
    |> AccountRepository.getSpendingPoolAccounts()
    |> Sum balances = totalBalance
  |> GetPendingTransactionsUseCase.execute()
    |> TransactionRepository.getPendingAndOverdue()
    |> Sum amounts = pendingReservations
  |> GetCreditCardReservedUseCase.execute()
    |> TransactionRepository.getCCPendingExpenses()
    |> Sum = creditCardReserved
  |> availableToSpend = totalBalance - pendingReservations - overdueReservations
  |> Emit to BudgetUiState

// Daily Velocity Computation Flow (pure, no DB)
Trigger: HeroSafeToSpend composition (runs on every recomposition with fresh date)
  |> DateUtils.daysRemainingInMonth(today)
    |> lastDayOfMonth - today.dayOfMonth + 1
  |> dailyRate = availableToSpend / daysRemainingInMonth
  |> isLastDay = daysRemainingInMonth == 1
  |> Render DailyVelocityRow(dailyRate, daysRemainingInMonth, isLastDay)

// Monthly Rollover Flow
Trigger: End of month (or first of next month)
  |> CalculateMonthEndBalance()
    |> Safe to Spend at month end
    |> Minus confirmed expenses
    |> = rolloverAmount
  |> SaveRolloverUseCase.execute(year, month, rolloverAmount)
    |> MonthlyRolloversRepository.insert()
  |> Emit updated BudgetSummary

// Sandbox Safe to Spend Flow
Trigger: Sandbox mode active
  |> CalculateSpendingPoolUseCase.execute(sandboxId)
    |> GetSandboxAccounts
    |> GetSandboxTransactions
    |> Apply same deduction logic
  |> Return sandbox-specific BudgetSummary
  // Note: Daily velocity is NOT shown in sandbox mode
```

---

## Function Signatures

```sudolang
fn CalculateSpendingPool(ctx: Context, includeSandbox: Boolean?): Result<SpendingPool>
fn GetBudgetSummary(ctx: Context, date: Long?): Result<BudgetSummary>
fn GetSafeToSpend(ctx: Context, includeSandbox: Boolean?): Result<Long>
fn GetCreditCardReservations(ctx: Context): Flow<List<CreditCardReservation>>
fn SaveRollover(ctx: Context, year: Int, month: Int, amount: Long): Result<MonthlyRollover>
fn GetRolloverHistory(ctx: Context, fromYear: Int, fromMonth: Int): Flow<List<MonthlyRollover>>
fn GetRolloverForMonth(ctx: Context, year: Int, month: Int): Result<MonthlyRollover?>
fn ApplyRolloverToSafeToSpend(ctx: Context): Result<Long>
fn CalculateProjectedSafeToSpend(ctx: Context, futureDate: Long): Result<Long>

// New utility (DateUtils.kt)
fn daysRemainingInMonth(today: LocalDate, timeZone: TimeZone = currentSystemDefault()): Int
  // Returns lastDayOfMonth - today.dayOfMonth + 1
  // Range: 1..31. Never returns 0 or negative.
```

---

## UI Components

```sudolang
// HeroSafeToSpend Props (updated)
interface HeroSafeToSpendProps {
  amount: Long;                // STS in cents
  dailyRate: Long?;            // Computed by caller: amount / daysRemainingInMonth; null if amount <= 0
  daysRemaining: Int?;         // Passed alongside dailyRate so component can render "· X days left"
  isLastDayOfMonth: Boolean;   // true when daysRemaining == 1; triggers warning state
  label: String;               // Default: "SAFE TO SPEND"
  modifier: Modifier;
}

// Layout — stacked Column:
//
//   SAFE TO SPEND                    ← label: bodyMedium, SemiBold, letterSpacing 2sp, textSecondary
//   ₱10,275.00                       ← displayMedium, textPrimary
//   ₱685.00 / day · 15 days left    ← bodyMedium, Light, textSecondary (normal state)
//
// Last day of month warning state:
//   ⚠ ₱10,275.00 / day · 1 day left ← color-error, icon = lucide AlertCircle (16dp), tappable
//   └─ Tooltip on tap: "You have 1 day left this month. This is your full Safe to Spend balance."
//      Tooltip style: 8CxCm — bg-dark, radius-md, 12pt, text-inverted

// DailyVelocityRow visual spec:
//   Normal:  typography.bodyMedium, fontWeight Light, color = textSecondary
//   Warning: typography.bodyMedium, fontWeight Light, color = color-error
//            + AlertCircle icon (16dp, color-error) leading the text
//            + clickable (showTooltip toggle)
//   Tooltip: inline Popup/Box anchored below the daily rate row
//            Text: "You have 1 day left this month. This is your full Safe to Spend balance."

// References design system:
// - 0yIOD (Hero / Safe to Spend) — base component being modified
// - 8CxCm (Tooltip) — used for last-day warning tooltip
```

---

## Database Schema

```sudolang
table monthly_rollovers {
  id: Long PK AUTOINCREMENT
  year: Integer NOT NULL
  month: Integer NOT NULL
  rollover_amount: Integer NOT NULL
  created_at: Integer NOT NULL
  UNIQUE(year, month)
}

table credit_card_settings {
  id: Long PK AUTOINCREMENT
  account_id: Long NOT NULL REFERENCES accounts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
  credit_limit: Integer
  statement_balance: Integer
  due_date: Integer
  created_at: Integer NOT NULL
  updated_at: Integer NOT NULL
}

index idx_rollovers_year_month ON monthly_rollovers(year, month)
index idx_cc_settings_account ON credit_card_settings(account_id)

// No schema changes for daily velocity — purely a display computation.
```

---

## SQLDelight Queries

```sudolang
// getTotalSpendingPoolBalance :one
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(balance), 0) FROM accounts WHERE is_in_spending_pool = 1;

// getSafeToSpendAmount :one
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(t.amount), 0) 
FROM transactions t
JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id
WHERE a.is_in_spending_pool = 1 
AND t.status IN ('pending', 'confirmed')
AND t.is_sandbox = 0;

// getCreditCardReservedAmount :one
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(t.amount), 0) 
FROM transactions t
JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id
WHERE a.type = 'credit_card'
AND t.status IN ('pending', 'confirmed')
AND t.is_sandbox = 0;

// getPendingTransactionsForSpendingPool :many
SELECT t.* FROM transactions t
JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id
WHERE a.is_in_spending_pool = 1 
AND t.status IN ('pending', 'overdue')
AND t.is_sandbox = 0
ORDER BY t.date ASC;

// getRolloverByMonth :one
SELECT * FROM monthly_rollovers WHERE year = :year AND month = :month;

// getAllRollovers :many
SELECT * FROM monthly_rollovers ORDER BY year DESC, month DESC;

// insertRollover :one
INSERT INTO monthly_rollovers (year, month, rollover_amount, created_at)
VALUES (:year, :month, :rollover_amount, :created_at)
RETURNING *;

// getCreditCardReservations :many
SELECT a.id as account_id, a.name, COALESCE(SUM(t.amount), 0) as reserved
FROM accounts a
LEFT JOIN transactions t ON t.account_id = a.id 
  AND t.status IN ('pending', 'overdue')
  AND t.type = 'expense'
  AND t.is_sandbox = 0
WHERE a.type = 'credit_card'
GROUP BY a.id, a.name;
```

## Dependencies

```sudolang
// External Dependencies
Dependency: sqldelight (2.0.1) - Database
Dependency: koin (3.5.3) - DI
Dependency: kotlinx-coroutines (1.7.3) - Async
Dependency: kotlinx-datetime (0.5.0) - Date handling (daysRemainingInMonth uses LocalDate)

// Internal Dependencies
Dependency: features/accounts - Query accounts where isInSpendingPool == true
Dependency: features/transactions - Query PENDING, OVERDUE transactions
Dependency: features/sandbox - sandbox_id parameter for sandbox calculation
Dependency: features/calendar - Safe to Spend header integration
Dependency: core/utils/DateUtils - daysRemainingInMonth() utility
Dependency: core/designsystem/component/HeroSafeToSpend - updated layout and warning state
```

---

## Edge Cases

| Case | Condition | Handling |
|------|-----------|----------|
| No accounts in pool | isInSpendingPool == false for all | Show $0.00 with explanation; daily rate not shown |
| All pending cancelled | pending_total == 0 | Full balance available |
| Month boundary | Calculation on last day | daysRemainingInMonth == 1; show warning state |
| Negative Safe to Spend | Deductions exceed balance | Show $0.00 with warning; daily rate not shown (dailyRate is null when amount <= 0) |
| Sandbox with no transactions | Only copied real data | Show same as real; no daily velocity in sandbox |
| Credit card with no pending | reserved == 0 | Show $0 reserved |
| Multiple credit cards | user > 1 CC | Show per-CC amounts in breakdown |
| Rollover calculation error | Failed to save rollover | Retry with exponential backoff |
| February 28 vs 29 | Leap year | kotlinx-datetime handles this correctly via LocalDate arithmetic |
| Months with 28/29/30/31 days | Any month | daysRemainingInMonth uses lastDayOfMonth from kotlinx-datetime — no hardcoding |
